body.title: Calibre device shim: MCP bus interface to Calibre content server
body.result: CalibreDevice + CalibreShim via calibredb; no SQLite; 11 tests; Igor migration deferred (TheIgors)
body.description:
Add a rack device for Calibre so Igor and other devices can query the book library via MCP tools instead of bypassing the rack with direct file/SQLite reads. The shim uses the Linux calibredb CLI (subprocess with --with-library) to interact with the library at /media/akien/onedrive/AkiensMedia/Ebooks/Calibre Library. No SQLite import in shim code; calibredb is an external binary like rg or git.

**Prerequisite:** Linux calibre must be installed (sudo apt install calibre or https://calibre-ebook.com/download_linux). calibredb must be in PATH. Shim config sets library_path; calibredb handles all DB access internally.

MCP tools to expose:
- search_books(query, limit=20) — calibredb search, returns ranked list of {id, title, authors, formats, tags}
- get_book_metadata(book_id) — full metadata for one book (title, authors, publisher, date, tags, comments, formats available)
- list_books(author=None, tag=None, limit=50) — browse by author or tag filter via calibredb list
- get_book_content(book_id, format='epub') — returns file path for the requested format if available

Config template includes library_path (default: /media/akien/onedrive/AkiensMedia/Ebooks/Calibre Library) and calibredb_path (default: calibredb, override if not in PATH). Shim falls back gracefully when calibredb is not found or library is unreachable — all tools return structured error.

**Migration:** Igor's existing direct Calibre access (learner.py direct calibredb/SQLite reads, scan_ebooks, calibre_catalog.py) is removed in this same sprint — replaced by calls to mcp__calibre__* tools. Igor loses his bypass path; the shim becomes the only interface. The calibre-sqlite-exempt audit allowlist entry (T-audit-sqlite-exempt-calibre) is also removed.

**Note:** Not all books in /media/akien/onedrive/AkiensMedia/Ebooks are in Calibre — only those imported into the Calibre Library. The shim exposes only what Calibre knows about.

**Affected files:** devices/calibre/ (new — device.py, shim.py, config.cfg), skeleton/registry.py (register new device), TheIgors/wild_igor/igor/tools/learner.py (remove direct calibre access), TheIgors/wild_igor/igor/tools/scan_ebooks.py (remove), TheIgors/wild_igor/igor/tools/calibre_catalog.py (remove or gut), audit_check_sqlite_imports.py (remove calibre exemption)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite (calibredb is a subprocess binary, not a sqlite3 import); device.py and shim.py inherit BaseDevice/BaseShim; config-driven library_path (not hardcoded); subprocess calls follow existing rg/git patterns
**Scope boundary:** IN: calibredb subprocess wrapper, 4 MCP tools above, config for library_path, graceful unavailable handling, Igor migration. OUT: replacing or managing calibre application itself, DRM stripping, format conversion, writing back to Calibre library, full-text content indexing (T-librarian-index-paths), books not in Calibre library.
**Completion criteria:** mcp__calibre__search_books(query='Pratchett') returns books from the library; get_book_metadata returns correct title/authors for a known book_id; shim returns structured error when calibredb not found; device registers in skeleton registry; Igor no longer has direct Calibre file access; 5+ tests covering search, metadata, unavailable-calibredb path
